milestone_brief: Close the deferred F-M2 finding from qtawesome-icons-2026q2-e2: migrate the Wireframe and Show-edges display toggles in AppearancePanel from QCheckBox+setIcon() to QPushButton(checkable=True). The current QCheckBox.setIcon() produces a [check-square][icon][label] triple-prefix that no peer scientific-viz app uses (Blender, ParaView, 3D Slicer all use icon-bearing checkable QPushButton or plain text checkboxes — never the triple-prefix). The migration eliminates the visual ambiguity (researcher unsure which affordance to click — the check-square or the icon) and aligns with industry convention. Architecture: replace self._wireframe_cb (QCheckBox) and self._edges_cb (QCheckBox) with QPushButton(checkable=True) instances. The icon becomes the primary affordance; the pressed/depressed visual state communicates checked-ness. The migration touches: (a) appearance_panel.py:_build_toggles_group widget construction, (b) _on_wireframe_toggled / _on_edges_toggled signal connections (QPushButton uses 'toggled' signal — same name as QCheckBox, so wiring stays the same), (c) appearance_panel.py:refresh_icons (no API change — setIcon works identically on both widget types), (d) styles.py QSS rules for the checked state in both PALETTE_LIGHT and PALETTE_DARK (this is the non-trivial part — needs a checked-state visual treatment that's visually distinct from hover and from unchecked in both themes, while maintaining WCAG 3:1 non-text contrast for the active state indicator). AI invariants: AI-9 (no processEvents added — setCheckable is synchronous), AI-11 (any new Qt enum references fully-qualified), AI-12 (checked-state background/border must clear 3:1 against BG_PANEL in both themes — this is the only meaningful contrast question), AI-13 (any new palette tokens 6-digit hex). The researcher should propose: (1) the QSS checked-state visual treatment (likely a subtle inset/depressed appearance plus a colored indicator stripe or filled background), (2) whether a new palette token like BG_TOGGLE_CHECKED is needed or can be assembled from existing tokens, (3) how tests/test_styles_palette.py guards the new checked-state contrast, (4) how test_appearance_panel (or similar) verifies the widget-type change doesn't break the toggled-signal contract. CONTEXT.md should gain an §8 entry recording the migration pattern (QCheckBox vs checkable QPushButton — when does each apply). Surface preservation: the actual wireframe/show-edges BEHAVIOR (apply_to_actor calls actor.prop.style and show_edges) must not change — this is purely a widget-type substitution at the UI level.
research_synthesis: Migrate Wireframe + Show-edges from QCheckBox+setIcon (triple-prefix) to QPushButton(checkable=True) with QSS role-property targeting. Reuse FOCUS_RING for the WCAG-compliant 2px checked-border (3.56:1 light / 5.17:1 dark — already proven). New BG_TOGGLE_CHECKED token per theme (#d4e6f5 light / #1a3048 dark) for the optional fill-tint reinforcement. Reuse BG_CAMERA_BTN_HOVER / BORDER_CAMERA_BTN for hover state. Tests via source-text grep (AI-2 compliant). Signal wiring unchanged — toggled(bool) is QAbstractButton-inherited.
